a:r.a:t: Key Gaps Identified
Impact
Limited earnings stability with low base pay
Pay structures with heavy variable earnings
can make roles
less attractive
, particularly in a seasonal business, and lead to higher turnover
Lack of formal career pathing and recognition structure
Limited progression and recognition mechanisms
can make it
difficult to retain strong performers
and create a clear path for development over time
Inconsistent and potentially overly aggressive target setting
Compensation plans are structured similarly to the market with clear upside, however based on employee and talent acquisition feedback,
targets might be set too high
, lowering actual earnings
Administrative complexities limits success of SPIFs and STIs
Reliance on short-term incentives can help drive day-to-day behaviors, but
delayed payment timing
(2 weeks after)
and administrative approvals limit flexibility and can reduce impact
Excessive commission tiers and plan complexity
Too many tiers and mechanics
make plans hard to understand, more complex to administer, and difficult for sellers to see a clear link between performance and pay
Limited use of profitability metrics in Director+ Plans
Director+ plans focus mainly on net sales volume, whereas the
market focuses on profitability
with the primary metric being NOI
The overall assessment unveiled several key gaps across compensation levels and design
Compensation Design & Market Assessment
